item.title: Eradicating child labour must be national priority
item.description: THE failure to eliminate child labour by the government’s own deadlines is a shame. The latest Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics survey on child labour reveals that 17.8 lakh children remain trapped in labour, with 10.7 lakh engaged in......

item.title: Cry for electricity

item.title: Bangladesh, a river civilisation
item.description: BANGLADESH was not born from stone. Bangladesh was born from water. Every year, rivers break this land apart, and those same rivers build new land again. People sow seeds again. They build homes again. They sing again. They love again. They begin again. This rebirth is Bangladesh’s first identity. The true culture of Bangladesh was not born in a palace. It was not......

item.title: Politics of institutional silence
item.description: PUBLIC institutions exist to serve citizens, not merely to administer them. Their legitimacy depends not only on how they perform their duties but also on how openly they account for them. Yet in Bangladesh, a journalist approaching a government office or autonomous institution with a camera is often met with a familiar response from the very official appointed to communicate with the public: ‘Speaking on camera is not possible.’......

item.title: Germany have higher ambitions than advancing to knockout stage: Nagelsmann
item.description: Coach Julian Nagelsmann was thrilled by Germany's comeback victory against Ivory Coast on Saturday, but stopped short of celebrating his side advancing to the World Cup knockout stage for the first time since 2014....
